summary: GZip compression and S3 photo storage features verified working. Backend: 10/10 tests pass. GZip middleware returns content-encoding: gzip headers. Photo upload to S3 returns /api/photos/... URLs. Photo serving endpoint works correctly. Frontend loads without errors.

gzip_compression.details: GZipMiddleware with minimum_size=500 bytes. Both /api/health and /api/estates return content-encoding: gzip header.

photo_upload_to_s3.details: PUT /api/auth/profile-photo uploads to S3, returns /api/photos/users/{id}/{uuid}.jpg URL instead of data: URL

photo_serving.details: GET /api/photos/{key:path} proxies from S3, returns image/jpeg with proper Cache-Control headers

photo_removal.details: Sending empty photo_data clears photo_url from user profile and deletes from S3

photo_url_resolution.details: resolve_photo_url in backend and resolvePhotoUrl in frontend correctly handle: data: URLs (passthrough), http URLs (passthrough), /api/photos/... paths (resolved), photos/... keys (converted to API path)

login_flow.details: Login with info@carryon.us / Demo1234! works. OTP bypass via demo code 000000 functional.

frontend_loading.details: Homepage loads correctly, login redirects to /admin dashboard, no console errors

critical_code_review_comments: GZipMiddleware correctly added at line 254 in server.py with minimum_size=500
photo_storage.py correctly processes images (resize, optimize) before S3 upload
photo_urls.py resolve_photo_url handles all URL formats correctly
Frontend utils/photoUrl.js mirrors backend logic for client-side resolution
PhotoPicker, BeneficiariesPage, OrbitVisualization all use resolvePhotoUrl for img src
seed_data_creation: None required - used existing admin account
context_for_next_testing_agent: GZip compression and S3 photo storage fully working. S3 bucket: carryon-vault (us-east-2). Photos uploaded via /api/auth/profile-photo, served via /api/photos/{key:path}. GZipMiddleware compresses responses >500 bytes. Frontend uses resolvePhotoUrl utility to convert storage keys to full URLs.
